question: Your new-to-you 1952 Cessna 150 burns a bit of oil. After reviewing the logs, you've decided to find out exactly how much oil has been put through your engine. It turns out that 216 pints have been added over the years. How many kilograms is that?

The tail tie-down is 1.07 m above the asphalt. If you add 24 inches for tying knots, how much total rope do you need to buy?

According to your recently discovered actual position, you have covered 31 nm. You are 4.2 nm right of course and you want to re-intercept your plotted course 15 nm from your destination. How many degrees do you need to turn?

question: The world's fastest recorded bird, a peregrine falcon named Frightful, was recorded traveling 242 MPH in a dive. If you were descending next to Frightful on the record-setting dive, what would your vertical speed indicator read?
